benchmark: synthetic numerical digital-twin manuscript claim stress test

claims.claim_text: Sparse-port full-field recovery is non-identifiable in the current one-dimensional benchmark.
claims.forbidden_overclaim: Sparse port data uniquely recover delta_T, c_v, m, and sigma.
claims.limitation: This proves an identifiability boundary for the synthetic benchmark, not a theorem for all devices.
claims.strongest_numerical_result: mean_sigma remains the dominant terminal-observable correlate; summary keys=['benchmark', 'best_lag_correlation_to_G', 'field_dynamic_ranges', 'hidden_field_correlations', 'input_path', 'interpretation']

claims.claim_text: gamma_sub recovery is conditionally possible under bounded T_sw prior.
claims.forbidden_overclaim: gamma_sub is identifiable under arbitrary released nuisance parameters.
claims.limitation: The claim requires fixed or tightly bounded switching/conductivity priors.

claims.claim_text: Wide T_sw mismatch is the dominant failure mode.
claims.forbidden_overclaim: Model mismatch does not affect gamma_sub inversion.
claims.limitation: Other parameters also confound gamma_sub; T_sw is the strongest tested one, not the only risk.

claims.claim_text: ltp_ltd and short_pulse style protocols improve recoverability relative to less informative choices.
claims.forbidden_overclaim: Any multi-protocol design automatically solves gamma_sub/T_sw ambiguity.
claims.limitation: Protocol rankings are response-surface guidance and need stronger validation before experimental prescription.

claims.claim_text: Naive weighted protocol objective does not improve over the best single protocol.
claims.forbidden_overclaim: Weighted stimulation is useless in general.
claims.limitation: Only tested configured weights; better optimized designs remain future work.

claims.claim_text: Response-surface phase diagrams are acceptable only with anchor verification and explicit qualification.
claims.forbidden_overclaim: All dense response-surface points are simulator-backed ODE solves.
claims.limitation: Dense profile is interpolated from simulator-backed source points, not thousands of fresh ODE solves.

claims.claim_text: F-SPS-PINN does not currently support a superiority claim.
claims.forbidden_overclaim: F-SPS-PINN is validated as more accurate than the free log_sigma shortcut.
claims.limitation: F-SPS remains an appendix/future-work method-development line.
